questions.text: Synthetic organic chemicals can cause which of the following problems:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ozone is produced in most of the tropical regions by the photochemical reactions of oxygen, from where it is transported to polar regions. It acts as a pollutant and causes various health problems i.e., damages eyes and aggravates asthma, decreases the elasticity of lung tissues, coughing, chest discomfort, etc.

questions.text: Which of the following is considered as cause of pollution:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Rapid increase in population leads to increase in need of basic resources like food and shelter leading to burning of more fossil fuels, deforestation etc which cause pollution.Harmful chemicals produced from industry and transportation are a leading cause of pollution.

questions.text: Which of the following is primary pollutant:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The waste products given out from chimneys of industrial units and exhaust of automobiles may contain gases such as sulphur dioxide, sulphur trioxide, nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons, ammonia, compounds of fluorine and radioactive materials. These waste products are called primary pollutants.

questions.text: In which of the following layer of atmosphere there is more thickness of ozone:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ozone, O3, is a gas having low boiling point. It is present in small concentrations throughout the atmosphere.The amount of ozone in the atmosphere is expressed in Dobson units (DU). The normal amount of overhead ozone is about 350 DU. The ozone layer, 25 - 28 km high, in the stratosphere surrounds the globe and filters most of the harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays in the sunlight before they could reach on the earth.

questions.text: Which of the following air pollutants is quiet killer:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide is highly poisonous gas and causes suffocation if inhaled. It binds blood haemoglobin more strongly than oxygen thus excluding oxygen from normal respiration. The CO poisoning can be reversed by giving high pressure oxygen. Exposure to high concentration of CO results in headache, fatigue, unconsciousness and eventually death (if such exposure is sustained for longer period). It is a colourless and odourless gas hence there is no way to detect it in case of leakage etc. so it is called quiet killer.

questions.text: The cause of water pollution is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The human activities such as livestock waste, landfills, agriculture, pesticides, oil leaks and spills, disposal of industrial effluents on open land, water bodies, septic tanks, detergents, mining, petroleum and natural gas production may result in the contamination of the surface and ground waters.

questions.text: The pH value of the acid rain is in the range of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The pH of unpolluted rain water should be 5.6.The rain water has pH less than 5 is considered truly acidic.

questions.text: Which of the following factors help to measure quality of water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: 1. Dissolved Oxygen (DO)In water the most important oxidizing agent is dissolved molecular oxygen (O2) the concentration of which ranges from 4 - 8 ppm. The organic matter is oxidized with the help of this dissolved oxygen in water.It is a parameter to determine the quality of water. 2. Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD)It is the capacity of organic matter in natural water to consume oxygen within a period of five days. The value of BOD is the amount of oxygen consumed as a result of biological oxidation of dissolved organic matter in the sample. 3. Chemical Oxygen Demand (COD)The organic content of water which consumes oxygen during chemical oxidation is evaluated by its chemical oxygen demand. The oxygen demand of water can be determined directly by treating it with dichromate ions Cr2O7 2- which is a powerful oxidizing agent.

questions.text: Which method is used to remove permanent hardness of water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The surface or ground water may also contain calcium and magnesium salts which make the water hard. The hard water is then appropriately treated to remove Ca2+ and Mg2+.

questions.text: To avoid, formation of toxic compounds with chlorine which of the followings is better to use as a disinfectant of water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Chlorine is frequently used to disinfect water. Chlorine treatment is very effective in killing the pathogens that may cause serious water-borne diseases such as typhoid and cholera. Chlorination of water containing organic materials also forms some organic compounds which are toxic. To avoide the formation of toxic compounds with chlorine, ozone or chlorine dioxide is used for the disinfection of water.

questions.text: Peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN) is an irritant to human beings and it affects:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: PANs have many adverse effects in the human body such as reduced respiratory function (including emphysema and impaired breathing) and eye irritation.

questions.text: A single chlorine free radical can destroy how many ozone molecules:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A single chloride free radical can destroy upto 100,000 ozone molecules.

questions.text: Ecosystem is a smaller unit of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Biosphere is the region of earth capable of supporting life. It includes lower atmosphere, the oceans, rivers, lakes, soils and solid sediments that actively interchange materials with all types of living organisms i.e., human beings, animals and plants. Ecosystem is a smaller unit of biosphere which consists of community of organisms and their interaction with environment i.e., animals, plants and microorganisms which lie in a definite zone

questions.text: The coagulant used in the purification of potable water is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The materials which are suspended or present in the colloidal form in raw water are removed by coagulation. The coagulant such as aluminium sulphate or alum is added to the raw water, which causes the precipitation of suspended impurities.

questions.text: Newspaper can be recycled again and again, by how many times:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The largest item which is recycled is newspaper and in its recycling process the release of chlorine or other bleaching acids and organic solvents is significantly less as compared to formation of these compounds during the processing of virgin newspaper.To improve the whiteness of the recycled newspaper it is blended with the virgin newspaper or sometimes treated with peroxides and hydrosulphites. In recycling process the fibre of the newspaper becomes shorter so it can be recycled again and again for five times.

questions.text: Which of the following is not common domestic materials which are recycled:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In recycling some of the used or waste materials are not discarded after their initial use but are processed so that they can be used again. The purpose of recycling is to conserve sources such as raw material and energy. The volume of the waste is also much reduced by recycling of the materials.The most common domestic materials that are recycled are paper, plastic, glass and aluminium. Iron is not recycled.

questions.text: The normal amount of overhead ozone is about:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The amount of ozone in the atmosphere is expressed in Dobson units (DU). The normal amount of overhead ozone is about 350 DU.

questions.text: When ground water seeps in the landfill a mixture of dissolved, suspended and microbial contaminants is formed, this mixture is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The municipal solid waste is mainly disposed off by dumping it in a landfill. The landfill is a large hole in the ground or even a bare piece of land. When the landfill becomes full with waste it is covered by soil or clay. The ground water which seeps in the landfill and liquid from the waste itself all percholate through the refuse producing leachate. The leachate contains dissolved, suspended and microbial contaminants.

questions.text: The gas that binds strongly with hemoglobin is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin much more strongly than oxygen. Its presence keeps oxygen from binding to hemoglobin. Carbon monoxide is highly poisonous gas and causes suffocation if inhaled. It binds blood haemoglobin more strongly than oxygen thus excluding oxygen from normal respiration.

questions.text: The concentration of dissolved oxygen in water ranges from:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In water the most important oxidizing agent is dissolved molecular oxygen (O2) the concentration of which ranges from 4 - 8 ppm. The organic matter is oxidized with the help of this dissolved oxygen in water.It is a parameter to determine the quality of water. The dissolved oxygen value less than 4 ppm indicates that water is polluted.

questions.text: Fungicides are the pesticides which:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The most important and widely used pesticides are insecticides (which kill insects), herbicides (which kill undesired plants) and fungicides (which control the growth of fungus on the plant).

questions.text: The main pollutant of leather tanneries in the waste water is due to the salt of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Leather tanneries use large quantities of chromium (VI) salts for leather tanning. They are producing good variety of exportable leather, but only some units have the facility of waste water treatment by reducing Cr (VI) into trivalent state followed by alkaline precipitation of Cr (OH)3. The effluents are discharged onto the open land or put into the sewage system. These industries are the big source of chromium (VI) pollution in the environment. Chromium (VI) is highly toxic and is known to cause cancer.

questions.text: The temperature in the incinerator of industrial waste products has a range:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A general process of high temperature incineration system consists of a rotary kiln which accepts all types of wastes including liquid, solid or sludge. The wastes are burned at temperatures between 650° to 1100°C. Ash from the rotating chamber is collected at waste tank and the remaining liquid gaseous materials are passed to the secondary chamber. This chamber is non-rotating and hence the temperature range of 950° to 1300°C is maintained.

questions.text: Which part of atmosphere is near to the earth:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The five main layers are the troposphere (closest to earth), stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere, and exosphere(farthest from earth).

questions.text: Thickness of atmosphere is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The layer of gases surrounding the earth is called atmosphere. It consists of various gases in different proportions i.e., N2 (78%), O2 (21%), Ar (0.9 %), CO2 (0.03 %) and trace amounts of H2, O3, CH4, CO, He, Ne, Kr and Xe. It also contains varying amounts of water vapours. Its thickness is about 1000 km above the surface of the earth and half of its mass is concentrated in the lower 5.6 km.

questions.text: How much fresh water is used for domestic purpose:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Oceans contain 97% of earth’s water but because of high salt contents this water cannot be used for human consumption. The polar ice caps and glaciers consist of 2% of the earth’s total water supply. Only 1% of the total earth’s water resources are available as fresh water i.e., surface water; river, lake, stream and ground water. The fresh water is being used by agriculture (69%), industry (23%) and for domestic purposes (8%).

questions.text: The most abundant element in the earth crust is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The 99.5 % mass of the lithosphere is made of 11 elements, which are oxygen (~ 46.60 %), Si (~27.72 %), Al (8.13 %), Fe (5.0 %), Ca (3.63 %), Na (2.83 %), K (2.59 %), Mg (2.09 %) and Ti, H2 and P (total less than 1 %). The elements present in trace amounts (0.1 to 0.02 %) are C, Mn, S, Ba, Cl, Cr, F, Zr, Ni, Sr and V.

questions.text: The residence time of NO is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The residence time of NO and NO2 in the atmosphere are 4 and 3 days respectively.

questions.text: The mean residence time of methane (CH4) in atmosphere is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Another natural source of methane is the anaerobic decomposition of organic matter by bacteria in water sediments and in soils. Methane has a mean residence time of about 3 -7 years in the atmosphere.

questions.text: The yellow colour in photochemical smog is due to the presence of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The yellow colour in photochemical smog is due to the presence of nitrogen dioxide.

questions.text: Ozone depletion is mainly due to the reaction of ozone (O3) with:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbons used as refrigerants in air conditioning and in aerosol sprays are inert in the troposphere but slowly diffuse into stratosphere, where they are subjected to ultraviolet radiation generating ClO free radicals. Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) play an effective role in removing O3 in the stratosphere .A single chloride free radical can destroy upto 100,000 ozone molecules.

questions.text: In human liver which substance cause cancer:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Chloroform CHCl3 is formed when hypochlorous acid reacts with organic matter (humic acid) dissolved in water. Chloroform is suspected liver carcinogen and also has negative reproduction and development effects in humans. The risk of bladder and rectal cancer increases by drinking chlorinated water.

questions.text: The pH range of the truely acidic rain is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The pH of unpolluted rain water should be 5.6.The rain water has pH less than 5 is considered truly acidic.

questions.text: Peroxyacetylinitrate (PAN) is an irritant to human beings and it affects:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The overall result of photochemical smog in afternoon is the built up of oxidizing agents such as H2O2, HNO3, peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN) and ozone in the air. PAN is an eye irritant and is also toxic to plants.

questions.text: To avoid the formation of toxic compounds with chlorine which substance is used for disinfecting water:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: To avoide the formation of toxic compounds with chlorine, ozone or chlorine dioxide is used for the disinfection of water.

questions.text: A single chloride free radical can destroy how many ozone molecules:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A single chloride free radical can destroy upto 100,000 ozone molecules.

questions.text: The main pollutant of leather tanneries in the waste water is due the salt of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Leather tanneries are producing good variety of exportable leather, but only some units have the facility of waste water treatment by reducing Cr (VI) into trivalent state followed by alkaline precipitation of Cr (OH)3. The effluents are discharged onto the open land or put into the sewage system. These industries are the big source of chromium (VI) pollution in the environment. Chromium (VI) is highly toxic and is known to cause cancer.

questions.text: In purification of potable water the coagulant used is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The materials which are suspended or present in the colloidal form in raw water are removed by coagulation. The coagulant such as aluminium sulphate or alum is added to the raw water, which causes the precipitation of suspended impurities.

questions.text: The temperature in the non-rotating chamber in the incineration of industrial and hazardous waste process has a range:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A general process of high temperature incineration system consists of a rotary kiln which accepts all types of wastes including liquid, solid or sludge. The wastes are burned at temperatures between 650° to 1100°C. Ash from the rotating chamber is collected at waste tank and the remaining liquid gaseous materials are passed to the secondary chamber. This chamber is non-rotating and hence the temperature range of 950° to 1300°C is maintained. In this chamber organic molecules are completely destroyed.

questions.text: Newspaper can be recycled again and again by how many times:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The largest item which is recycled is newspaper and In recycling process the fibre of the newspaper becomes shorter so it can be recycled again and again for five times
